title: Listening: The Minister's Speech
learningGoal: Understand high-register Sanskritized Hindi and decode subjunctive structures indicating purpose and recommendation.
instructions: Listen to the education minister giving a formal speech. Pay close attention to the vocabulary he uses (it will not be the Hindi you hear on the street) and how he phrases his recommendations.
listeningGoal: Identify formal vocabulary (Shiksha, Samasya, Anurodh) and comprehend subjunctive purpose clauses (Taaki... sakein).
audioScenario: A formal address by a government minister on national television.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: मंत्री: नमस्कार। आज मैं शिक्षा की समस्या पर बात करूँगा।
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Mantri: Namaskar. Aaj main shiksha ki samasya par baat karunga.
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Minister: Hello. Today I will talk about the problem of education.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: मंत्री: यह एक बहुत महत्वपूर्ण विषय है।
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Mantri: Yeh ek bahut mahatvapoorna vishay hai.
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Minister: This is a very important subject.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: मंत्री: हमें चाहिए कि हम नए स्कूल खोलें।
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Mantri: Humein chahiye ki hum naye school kholein.
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Minister: We should open new schools. (Lit: It is needed to us that we may open new schools).

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: मंत्री: मेरा सभी शिक्षकों से अनुरोध है कि वे मेहनत करें।
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Mantri: Mera sabhi shikshakon se anurodh hai ki ve mehnat karein.
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Minister: It is my request to all teachers that they work hard.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: मंत्री: हम यह सब कर रहे हैं ताकि हमारे छात्र सफल हो सकें।
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Mantri: Hum yeh sab kar rahe hain taaki humare chhatra safal ho sakein.
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Minister: We are doing all this so that our students may be able to succeed.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: मंत्री: अब, यदि आपकी अनुमति हो, तो मैं अपना भाषण समाप्त करूँ।
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Mantri: Ab, yadi aapki anumati ho, toh main apna bhashan samapt karun.
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Minister: Now, if there is your permission, then may I conclude my speech.

audioTranscriptFull: मंत्री: नमस्कार। आज मैं शिक्षा की समस्या पर बात करूँगा। यह एक बहुत महत्वपूर्ण विषय है। हमें चाहिए कि हम नए स्कूल खोलें। मेरा सभी शिक्षकों से अनुरोध है कि वे मेहनत करें। हम यह सब कर रहे हैं ताकि हमारे छात्र सफल हो सकें। अब, यदि आपकी अनुमति हो, तो मैं अपना भाषण समाप्त करूँ। धन्यवाद।

questionSet.questionText: What does the minister say he will talk about?
options: The problem of education (Shiksha ki samasya).

questionSet.questionText: Listen to: 'Humein chahiye ki hum naye school kholein'. What grammar structure is this?
options: A formal recommendation using 'Chahiye ki' followed by the subjunctive 'kholein'.

questionSet.questionText: What formal word does the minister use to make a 'request' to the teachers?
Why are they opening schools and working hard? (Hint: Listen for 'Taaki')
options: So that their students may succeed (Taaki humare chhatra safal ho sakein).
So that the teachers get paid.
So that the speech can end.

questionSet.questionText: At the very end, how does the minister ask for permission to conclude?
options: He uses 'Anumati' (permission) and the subjunctive 'Samapt karun' (May I conclude).
He says 'Khatam karta hoon' (I am ending it).

correctAnswers.lq_1: The problem of education (Shiksha ki samasya).
correctAnswers.lq_2: A formal recommendation using 'Chahiye ki' followed by the subjunctive 'kholein'.
correctAnswers.lq_4: So that their students may succeed (Taaki humare chhatra safal ho sakein).
correctAnswers.lq_5: He uses 'Anumati' (permission) and the subjunctive 'Samapt karun' (May I conclude).

feedbackRules.onPass: Brilliant! You are ready to listen to Hindi news broadcasts and political debates.
feedbackRules.onFail: Listen closely to the endings of the verbs. 'Kholein', 'Karein', 'Sakein', 'Karun'. Notice how they do not end in 'ga' or 'ge'. This is the subjunctive mood.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Pay attention to the formal vocabulary. 'Vishay' means subject/topic. 'Shikshak' means teacher. 'Chhatra' means student.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: मंत्री: नमस्कार। आज मैं शिक्षा की समस्या पर बात करूँगा। यह एक बहुत महत्वपूर्ण विषय है। हमें चाहिए कि हम नए स्कूल खोलें। मेरा सभी शिक्षकों से अनुरोध है कि वे मेहनत करें। हम यह सब कर रहे हैं ताकि हमारे छात्र सफल हो सकें। अब, यदि आपकी अनुमति हो, तो मैं अपना भाषण समाप्त करूँ। धन्यवाद।

hintText: To understand formal Hindi, you must forget the English/Urdu words you learned on the street. 'Problem' is not 'Pareshani', it is 'Samasya'.
